name: Dasatinib plus quercetin reduced endothelial telomere dysfunction-induced foci in aged mice
summary: An aged-mouse senolytic intervention reduced telomere dysfunction markers in endothelial cells, supporting the Senolytics animal biomarker map while preserving the telomere-protection boundary.
statement: In old mice, intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin reduced endothelial p21 expression, DNA damage, and telomere dysfunction-induced foci despite reducing mean endothelial telomere length.
population_or_model: Old C57BL/6 mice treated with dasatinib plus quercetin or vehicle from 21 to 24 months of age
quantitative_note: The source reports 10 mice per group and approximately 57% lower endothelial p21 expression with D+Q; telomere length decreased by about 17% without producing critically short telomeres.
caveats: This is an aged-mouse senolytic result, not a direct shelterin or capping-restoration therapy.
The telomere-dysfunction signal may reflect removal of senescent cells rather than preservation of capping in surviving cells.
Mouse telomeres are longer than human telomeres, limiting translation of the telomere-length boundary.
